title: Chronic cough

red_flags.text: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped cough presentation scope within DHA telehealth.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped haemoptysis, weight loss, fever or night sweats, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

examination.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped chest, oropharyngeal, and nasal examination fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.
investigations.rationale: Was reviewed for investigation context; the more specific mapped chest-radiograph and spirometry fields use the BTS statement.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

investigations.text: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped chest radiography, spirometry, selected upper-airway referral, and clinician referral fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

assessment.text: Defines chronic cough in adults as cough lasting more than 8 weeks and sets the statement scope.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped adult chronic-cough identity and matching terms.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines chronic cough in adults as cough lasting more than 8 weeks and sets the statement scope.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

exact_section.heading: Scope and definition of chronic cough

medication.text: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped duration, character, sputum, timing, triggers, reflux or postnasal context, medicine and smoking history, and structured history.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

safety_netting.text: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports the mapped age-and-smoking-qualified new-cough warning as a prompt only.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

source_limitations: The legacy workflow does not enforce the adult, more-than-8-week, age, smoking, or telehealth qualifiers of the exact sources.

Voice change or stridor, persistent localized abnormality, oxygen saturation, respiratory rate, previous-investigation review, generic clinician plans, inhaled-therapy trials, fixed follow-up, PRN wording, and some generic safety-netting remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, cancer-risk interpretation, investigation selection, treatment, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
